incidentRecordSummaries.description: The details of each listed incident record.

nextToken.description: The pagination token to use when requesting the next set of items. If there are no additional items to return, the string is null.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the incident.

creationTime.description: The timestamp for when the incident was created.

impact.description: Defines the impact to customers and applications.

incidentRecordSource.description: What caused Incident Manager to create the incident.

invokedBy.description: The service principal that assumed the role specified in createdBy. If no service principal assumed the role this will be left blank.

resourceArn.description: The resource that caused the incident to be created.

source.description: The service that started the incident. This can be manually created from Incident Manager, automatically created using an Amazon CloudWatch alarm, or Amazon EventBridge event.

resolvedTime.description: The timestamp for when the incident was resolved.

title.description: The title of the incident. This value is either provided by the response plan or overwritten on creation.

nextToken.description: The pagination token to use when requesting the next set of items. If there are no additional items to return, the string is null.

replicationSetArns.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the list replication set.

nextToken.description: The pagination token to use when requesting the next set of items. If there are no additional items to return, the string is null.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the response plan.

displayName.description: The human readable name of the response plan. This can include spaces.

name.description: The name of the response plan. This can't include spaces.

errors.description: A list of errors encountered during the operation.

creationTime.description: The timestamp for when a finding was created.

cloudFormationStackUpdate.description: Information about the CloudFormation stack creation or update associated with the finding.

endTime.description: The timestamp for when the CloudFormation stack creation or update ended. Not reported for deployments that are still in progress.

stackArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the CloudFormation stack involved in the update.

startTime.description: The timestamp for when the CloudFormation stack creation or update began.

codeDeployDeployment.description: Information about the CodeDeploy deployment associated with the finding.

deploymentGroupArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the CodeDeploy deployment group associated with the deployment.

endTime.description: The timestamp for when the CodeDeploy deployment ended. Not reported for deployments that are still in progress.

startTime.description: The timestamp for when the CodeDeploy deployment began.

lastModifiedTime.description: The timestamp for when the finding was most recently updated with additional information.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the replication set.
The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the response plan.

eventId.description: The ID of the event for easy reference later.

incidentRecordArn.description: The ARN of the incident record that you added the event to.

incidentRecord.description: Details the structure of the incident record.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the incident record.

automationExecutions.description: The runbook, or automation document, that's run at the beginning of the incident.

chatChannel.description: The chat channel used for collaboration during an incident.

chatbotSns.description: The Amazon SNS targets that Chatbot uses to notify the chat channel of updates to an incident. You can also make updates to the incident through the chat channel by using the Amazon SNS topics.

empty.description: Used to remove the chat channel from an incident record or response plan.

creationTime.description: The timestamp for when Incident Manager created the incident record.

dedupeString.description: The string Incident Manager uses to prevent duplicate incidents from being created by the same incident in the same account.

impact.description:
The impact of the incident on customers and applications.

Supported impact codes

  • 1 - Critical

  • 2 - High

  • 3 - Medium

  • 4 - Low

  • 5 - No Impact

incidentRecordSource.description: Details about the action that started the incident.

invokedBy.description: The service principal that assumed the role specified in createdBy. If no service principal assumed the role this will be left blank.

resourceArn.description: The resource that caused the incident to be created.

source.description: The service that started the incident. This can be manually created from Incident Manager, automatically created using an Amazon CloudWatch alarm, or Amazon EventBridge event.

lastModifiedTime.description: The timestamp for when the incident was most recently modified.

notificationTargets.description: The Amazon SNS targets that are notified when updates are made to an incident.

resolvedTime.description: The timestamp for when the incident was resolved. This appears as a timeline event.

summary.description: The summary of the incident. The summary is a brief synopsis of what occurred, what's currently happening, and context of the incident.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the replication set.

createdBy.description: Details about who created the replication set.

deletionProtected.description: Determines if the replication set deletion protection is enabled or not. If deletion protection is enabled, you can't delete the last Amazon Web Services Region in the replication set.

lastModifiedTime.description: When the replication set was last updated.

regionMap.description: The map between each Amazon Web Services Region in your replication set and the KMS key that's used to encrypt the data in that Region.

status.description: The status of the replication set. If the replication set is still pending, you can't use Incident Manager functionality.

nextToken.description: The pagination token to use when requesting the next set of items. If there are no additional items to return, the string is null.

resourcePolicies.description: Details about the resource policy attached to the response plan.

policyDocument.description: The JSON blob that describes the policy.

ramResourceShareRegion.description: The Amazon Web Services Region that policy allows resources to be used in.

actions.description: The actions that this response plan takes at the beginning of the incident.

chatChannel.description: The Chatbot chat channel used for collaboration during an incident.

displayName.description: The long format name of the response plan. Can contain spaces.

engagements.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the contacts and escalation plans that the response plan engages during an incident.

incidentTemplate.description: Details used to create the incident when using this response plan.

integrations.description: Information about third-party services integrated into the Incident Manager response plan.

name.description: The short format name of the response plan. The name can't contain spaces.

ssmAutomation.description: The Systems Manager automation document to start as the runbook at the beginning of the incident.

documentVersion.description: The automation document's version to use when running.

dynamicParameters.description: The key-value pair to resolve dynamic parameter values when processing a Systems Manager Automation runbook.

parameters.description: The key-value pair parameters to use when running the automation document.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the role that the automation document will assume when running commands.

targetAccount.description: The account that the automation document will be run in. This can be in either the management account or an application account.

eventTime.description: The timestamp for when the event occurred.

eventType.description: The type of event that occurred. Currently Incident Manager supports only the Custom Event and Note types.

eventUpdatedTime.description: The timestamp for when the timeline event was last updated.

incidentRecordArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the incident that the event occurred during.

findings.description: A list of findings that represent deployments that might be the potential cause of the incident.

nextToken.description: The pagination token to use when requesting the next set of items. If there are no additional items to return, the string is null.

lastModifiedTime.description: The timestamp for when the finding was last updated.

nextToken.description: The pagination token to use when requesting the next set of items. If there are no additional items to return, the string is null.

generatedId.description:
A unique ID for a RelatedItem.

Don't specify this parameter when you add a RelatedItem by using the UpdateRelatedItems API action.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the related item, if the related item is an Amazon resource.

metricDefinition.description: The metric definition, if the related item is a metric in Amazon CloudWatch.

pagerDutyIncidentDetail.description: Details about an incident that is associated with a PagerDuty incident.

autoResolve.description: Indicates whether to resolve the PagerDuty incident when you resolve the associated Incident Manager incident.

id.description: The ID of the incident associated with the PagerDuty service for the response plan.

secretId.description: The ID of the Amazon Web Services Secrets Manager secret that stores your PagerDuty key, either a General Access REST API Key or User Token REST API Key, and other user credentials.

url.description: The URL, if the related item is a non-Amazon Web Services resource.

tags.description: A list of tags for the response plan or incident.

eventSummaries.description: Details about each event that occurred during the incident.

nextToken.description: The pagination token to use when requesting the next set of items. If there are no additional items to return, the string is null.

eventTime.description: The timestamp for when the event occurred.

eventType.description: The type of event. The timeline event must be Custom Event or Note.

eventUpdatedTime.description: The timestamp for when the timeline event was last updated.

incidentRecordArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the incident that the event happened during.
The ARN of the newly created incident record.
